entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model: What’s New as of 2025-03-03?
entry.content:
Hi there!  

We are back again for another What’s New for SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM). This month’s What’s New will focus on the enhancements in the Environment screen and the consolidation of Show and Analyze Screen.  

In case you missed it, you can always catch up on the latest developments and releases about SAP PaPM Cloud SM following the relevant tag: PaPMCloudWhatsNew 

Let’s start! 

Environments Screen Enhancements 

SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model has enhanced the Environments screen by replacing inline editing and drag-and-drop interactions with modal dialogs. This update streamlines actions like adding, editing, moving, and deleting environments, providing a more consistent and user-friendly experience. Additionally, automatic saving eliminates the need for manual save, undo, and redo actions. 

More details about the Environments screen enhancements can be found in the SAP PaPM Cloud blog post: SAP PaPM Cloud SM: Enhancements on the Environments Screen or visit our Application Help portal Environments screen. 

Consolidation of Show and Analyze Screen 

Two of the most frequently used screens in SAP PAPM Cloud Standard Model application, Show and Analyze screens, have been consolidated into one unique screen. Displaying and maintaining data in tabular view in Show screen and manipulating data in aggregated view in Analyze screen is now merged into single Data maintenance screen.  
For your reference, please visit Show screen and Analyze data sections in the Application Help portal. 

Access to the Consolidated Show & Analyze Screen 

Previously, authorized users could access and maintain data in the Show and Analyze screens through two different buttons.

With the new implementation, Show and Analyze screens are merged into one screen, which can be accessed via the Manage Data button.  

Upon accessing the Show screen of any function, the URL part isPivotModeOn states if the pivoted mode is enabled. In case authorized users wish to view and maintain data in tabular view they can keep the Pivot toggle disabled.

Once users wish to change the data representation from tabular to aggregated view, they can enable the Pivot toggle. 

Layouts for different pivot modes are separated, which means that the user can save different set of layouts for two different pivot modes: enabled and disabled.  

In case user makes any changes in the Show screen with disabled pivot mode , an asterisk indicator (*) will show up besides the current layout. Upon trying to enable the Pivot toggle, the user will get a warning message that unsaved changes for current mode will be discarded in case user chooses to leave the view. 

The same behavior will be applicable if the user tries to disable the Pivot toggle while they are in pivot mode with unsaved layout changes. 

That’s it for now! 
Stay tuned for more enhancements and exciting release in the next months’ What’s New. 
Until next time and see you next month!

entry.title: Re: Unable to add New Individual Customer on Lead Entity
entry.content:
Individual Customer is now available for Lead entity 🙂 We could create Lead A directly from Individual Customer B view now.

After creation, then the Individual Customer B will be visible under the Individual Customer section in the Lead A.

But from Lead view, we are not able to create new Individual Customer there. This is the same behaviour for Account.

You could also check it in the attached help document: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/help/23ec04ee830846958605622f27ff5c3d/9a74b3db556e4f74a5ae3dd500bfac0c.html?locale=en-US&parentHref=/whats-new/58f0cc34e3c141a793ba8dfb7fc47f21?locale=en-US&parentName=What%27s+New+in+SAP+Sales+Cloud+Version%C2%A02


Thank you!

Best Regards,
Molly

entry.title: 嵌入式 SAC 客戶用例分享之加值中心集成（台灣地區本地化需求）
entry.content:
本文介紹的台灣特有的業務場景：透過加值中心集成申報 VAT。這是客戶系統實施嵌入式分析的一次概念驗證的成果，以驗證可以在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中獲取到台灣 VAT 申報所需要集成的相關電子發票資訊，並支持將其資料可視化。本文以台灣某加值中心的集成需求為例，向讀者介紹如何借助嵌入式分析獲取到相關業務資料，支持客戶進行集成。

客戶的需求

台灣地區客戶可透過自選的加值中心進行VAT申報。在系統導入專案的實施過程中，其面臨的主要挑戰是抽取並整合來自不同交易憑證的資料源。由於各個加值中心在格式和欄位標準上存在差異，目前 SAP 並未提供與各加值中心廠商的開箱即用集成解決方案。因此，客戶需要充分利用現有的 CDS 視圖，透過客製化開發，獲取所需的資料，並滿足特定格式要求，實現資料的高效集成。

鑒於上述挑戰，客戶可考慮採用嵌入式分析功能來滿足該業務需求。

本文將基於以下 VAT 申報集成欄位需求示例展開：

元素順序

元素名稱

1

M

M

8

O

6

6

O

7

8

O

8

發票接收時間

8

O

9

12

M

10

M

M

M

O

10

彙開標記

解決方案

主要步驟：

  • 分析業務需求，確立 S/4HANA Cloud 中對應的資料來源 CDS 及欄位名
  • 針對尚未 Release 的標準 CDS，創建客製化義業務對象（Custom Business Object）
  • 創建客製化 CDS 視圖
  • 創建客製化分析查詢
  • 創建多维報表

具體步驟將在下面詳細描述。為了保護客戶敏感資料，一些截圖來自虛擬測試系統。

  1. 分析業務需求，確立 S/4HANA Cloud 中對應的資料來源 CDS 及欄位

當前 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 暂未提供整合的電子發票 CDS 視圖，故專案實施需要透過各個模組的憑證資料來源 CDS 來獲取相關資料，並加工整合。

  • 以前文提到的業務需求為例，識別出以下核心標準 CDS 視圖作為資料來源：
  • CDS：I_BillingDocument、I_BillingDocumentItem 覆蓋資料：基於請款憑證的發票抬頭及行明細資訊
  • CDS：I_Address_2 覆蓋資料：發票人與受票人的名稱、電話、地址等資訊
  • CDS：I_Businesspartnertaxnumber 覆蓋資料：受票人的統一編號資訊
  • CDS：I_KR_BusinessPlace 覆蓋資料：發票人的營業場所詳細資訊
  • 需要特殊處理的數值
  • 稅率別

依照請款憑證行項目的稅率分為：1 應稅、2 零稅率、3 免稅。從系統中的稅碼到稅分類的轉換，需要在 CDS 數值加工中處理，詳見步驟3。

  • 營業場所與銷售辦公室的關係

發票人的營業場所資訊（例如發票人統一編號、印章電話等）需要透過請款憑證行項目的 Sales Office 欄位讀取其對應的 Business Place 資訊。但當前系統中暂無 Sales Office 與 Business Place 關聯關係的 CDS，故此關係需要借助客製化業務對象（Custom Business Object）來維護實現，詳見步驟2。

除本文提供的實施建議外，若您期望 SAP 能透過標準產品內容提供統整的台灣電子發票 CDS 視圖，歡迎您透過贵司的 S-User 訪問 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/340825，投出您寶貴的一票。

   2. 針對尚未 Release 的標準 CDS，創建客製化義業務對象（Custom Business Object） 

由於目前沒有現成的 CDS 視圖展示 Business Place 和 Sales Office 的關聯關係，可以創建一個客製化業務對象 YY1_BUSINESSPLACE2SALESOFF。該對象將保存 Business Place 和 Sales Office 之間的對應關係，暴露相關資料。

 

本文假定業務場景為，台灣地區設台北、高雄各一個營業場所。在客製化義業務對象中維護其對應關係如下。 注意該資料資訊應與配置點 103391 Assign Business Places to Sales Offices 保持一致。

 

    3. 創建客製化 CDS 視圖

 

接著需要創建 2 個 CDS 來揭露相關資料來源。找到應用客製化義 CDS 視圖並創建新的 CDS 視圖。

 

首先創建的是標準 CDS 視圖 YY1_BillingItemTW。該 CDS 視圖主要揭露了電子發票的詳細資訊，例如發票代碼、金額、發票人和受票人等資訊。選擇 I_BillingDocumentItem 作為主資料來源，並添加 I_BillingDocument、I_Businesspartnertaxnumber 以及客製化業務對象 YY1_BUSINESSPLACE2SALESOFF。

 

添加參數起始日期和截止日期。

 

 

添加主資料來源和關聯資料來源裡的元素，例如發票代碼、稅額、金額等。

 

添加必要的計算，例如稅率別。由於 I_TW_TaxCode 未開放用於開發，所以需要透過創建計算，按固定邏輯獲取配置級別的稅碼分類。本例中的計算邏輯如下：

A1/A2/A6/A9: 1

A3/A5/A7/AW: 2

A8/AZ: 3

 

維護元素屬性並設置過濾。

 

儲存並發布該視圖。

然後基於建立成功的CDS視圖YY1_BillingItemTW，建立第二個CDS視圖YY1_BillingCubeTW，並新增關聯資料來源I_KR_BusinessPlace和I_Address_2，主要用於揭露受票人和發票人的詳細資訊。該CDS視圖為cube類型。

 

新增必要的元素以及計算。例如受票人和發票人地址。透過建立計算，組合地址資訊，以滿足不同價值中心的格式需求。

 

維護元素屬性，然後儲存並發布 CDS 視圖。 到目前為止，所有視圖已建立完成。

   4. 建立自訂分析查詢

在主頁中找到「自訂分析查詢和服務」應用並建立新的客製化查詢。（⚠️“自訂分析查詢和服務”是新的建立分析查詢的應用程式，後續支援發布為Ina Service，用於後續MDA報表、審核手冊和eSAC故事。）

 

選擇YY1_BillingCubeTW作為資料來源並新增欄位。

 

 

然後儲存並發布查詢。

接下來需要將建立好的查詢發布為InA服務。進入管理InA服務。

 

添加查詢 YY1_BILLINGTW，然後發布。

  5. 建立多維報表

進入管理KPI與報表應用，選擇多維報表，點擊建立，添加建立好的查詢為資料來源。儲存並發布。

 

依照業務需求，為該報表維護業務目錄。多維報表建立完成。客戶可以依照自己的業務需求，調整頁面佈局及格式，增加顯示一些關鍵度量的圖表可視化。

 

結論

透過使用嵌入式分析，客戶可以高效地利用其強大的分析功能來解決業務上的分析問題。當然客戶也可以直接將CDS封裝為API進行消費。具体操作可參考鏈結。

在未來，我們將繼續與讀者分享更多有關嵌入式分析的成功案例。

有關SAP S/4HANA Cloud嵌入式SAC的更多資訊，可以查看以下鏈結：

  • S/4HANA Cloud嵌入式分析——数据展现的灵活扩展：click here
  • 嵌入式分析云客户用例：如何在S/4HANA Cloud中创建定制SAC story和dashboard: click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之生产成本分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之货物移动分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之库龄分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之物料库存分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之销售毛利分析：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之外向交货和开票概览：click here
  • 嵌入式SAC客户用例分享之交货完成分析：click here

I am following the steps mentioned in this tutorial.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-install-adt..html

I have the latest Eclipse for Java developers (2024-12).

When I try to install ADT using the HANA tools URL (https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest) it always fails at 16% with the error message shown in the screenshots.

I am using an office laptop. I have tried with and without admin access. Tried changing the network settings.

It seems it is able to download some of the packages but not all. So, I am a bit unsure how to fix this. Please see the attachments for some more details.

Any pointers will be helpful.

entry.title: Encountering "Error in MDDataSetBW.GetCellData" with InfoProvider ZISX002$N – Need Assistance
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am experiencing the following error in my SAP BW system:

The following database error occurred: Error in MDDataSetBW.GetCellData. Error reading the data of InfoProvider ZISX002$N. For information about this error, please refer to SAP Knowledge Base Article 2054721 on the SAP Support Portal. (IES 10901)

I have reviewed the referenced SAP Knowledge Base Article 2054721 but would appreciate further insights. Has anyone encountered this issue before? What troubleshooting steps or configuration adjustments helped resolve this error?

Trobleshooted Issues :

1. We have created index in the backend BW  and ran the Bex query running fine with out any issues

2. We re ran the report and it is getting success.

3. We are receiving intermediate issue with the above issue running in business objects

Data Source is SAP BW - Universe based reports (.unv format)

SAP BOBJ Version : 4.2 SP9

Thank you in advance for your assistance!

entry.title: SAC for Planning - DATEDIFF with variable-member or integer, how to?
entry.content:
Hello, 
I need to find out the difference between to dates. However these dates are not properties but actual income and outgoing transactions.

I separated them into 3 measures (income outgoing and difference), but I can't change these into accounts or create a generic dimension if that's the best practice. I have only one time dimension, also here I can create few time dimensions if that's the best practice.  let me know your thoughts.
 
in the below example the result I'm looking for is Jan 6th minus Jan 4th = 2 days 

I made several trials with INTEGER or VARIABLEMEMBER (like below) no one worked.
example of a failure:

VARIABLEMEMBER #INCOME OF [d/Date]
VARIABLEMEMBER #OUTCOME OF [d/Date]

FOREACH [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_STATUS]
    IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Income", [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_STATUS]="STS_023") > 0 THEN
       DATA([d/Measures]="Income",[d/Date]=#INCOME)=RESULTLOOKUP()
    ENDIF
    IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Outgoing", [d/IL_D_POC_RPD_STATUS]="STS_023") > 0 THEN
       DATA([d/Measures]="Outgoing", [d/Date]=#OUTCOME)=RESULTLOOKUP()
    ENDIF
ENDFOR

DATA([d/Measures]="Time_deviation",[d/Date]="2025-01-01") = DATEDIFF(#INCOME, #OUTCOME, "DAY")

 

thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP B1: Can I add a column to Inventory Status (Avail...
entry.content:
Hi,

Unfortunately you cannot add columns or user defined fields to reports. Using the UI API, you might be able to add a column, but that would likely make the report very slow.

Please consider the following possible solutions:

  • Check the Form settings, to see if the information you need is simply not shown.
  • This is a fairly simple report, you could create a query to return the same data as the system report, including the data that you want extra, and then create a report from it

     

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: RAP / Restful Application Programming Model: Projected association is not draft enabled
entry.content:
Hello all,

I am currently developing an RAP (Restful) App.

Relevant architecture: Managed scenario, draft enabled, multiple associations and multiple compositions are present. CRUD Operations have been mapped accordingly and strict( 2 ) is active for the Behavior Definition.

I first had an issue regarding the CREATE Button stemming from the managed scenario Behaviour definition, which did not generate. After some research it appeared to be based on the fact that for OData Version 4, CREATE and EDIT Buttons can only be generated provided draft is enabled.

Following that, I have just finished generating the Draft Tables necessary for all my compositions (which are used for multiple lineitem sections on the object page) and now come to an error message, which is hindering my progress.

The message is:

"Error in entity '(Projection_View_Name(CDS))': Projected association _AssociationName of Projection_View_Name is not draft enabled."
>>> Clicking into the message navigates to the Service Binding, which (since that message appeared) is not published anymore and also shows a error message next to its name.

Based on the message I would think, that I would have to specifically define the given association inside the Projection View to be draft enabled, but I am not aware of the syntax. How could this be achieved?

Thanks in advance for every response!

 

04.03.2025 - EDIT:
If the Behaviour Projection it supplied with the syntax "use association _AssociationName { with draft; }" then the message results in: "Error in entity 'Projection_View_Name(CDS)': Entity Projection_View_Name: Association _AssociationName cannot be draft enabled."

entry.title: Multiple SAP on-premise systems authentication and authorization to single web application
entry.content:
It has been a long time that I want to figure out how to do authentication from one web application (NodeJS) to multiple SAP on-premise systems (ERP, and S/4). So, by definition, I have developed a web application and I need to visualize data located on the SAP side. In other words, I want to use the SAP Side as the database layer and the web app as the presentation layer. The main challenge that I can't figure out is how to do the authentication and authorization to multiple SAP systems (at some points the system may be inside the customer network) from a single publicly hosted web application that is based on NodeJS. 

How do I want to use the SAP system after authentication and authorization?

1. Function call

2. Consuming CDS views

 

I can setup more services, but I don't have a concise knowledge of whether those services would be useful or not. E.g. SSO.

entry.title: Re: Candidate profile
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes we have a option to enable the functionality "Internal to External Candidate Profile conversion"
A conversion job analyzes the predefined conditions to convert the internal Employee Candidate Profile to External Candidate Profile.
When an employee leaves the organization, the employee’s user account is inactivated. The corresponding internal candidate profile is converted to external candidate profile. A deconversion process does the conversion from internal to external candidate profile.
Please check the below link to understand the functionality and steps to enable it 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RECRUITING/8477193265ea4172a1dda118505ca631/8fc6102279524094ae9c1c97eea4520a.html

Regards,
Arun Singh

entry.title: Integration of QR Code Scanning in SAP Module Pool Using HTML Service
entry.content:
Introduction:

This blog provides an extensive overview of the steps required to implement a Scan QR functionality in a module pool program by triggering an HTML QR service with a pushbutton and returning the retrieved data to the screen after scanning, ensuring seamless integration between the SAP module pool program and the HTML QR scanning service to enable efficient QR code processing.

Steps:

Step 1: Create a class using transaction se24 and specify IF_HTTP_EXTENSION in the interface section. This class is intended to handle HTTPS requests and responses in the context of web-based applications or services, particularly custom handlers in the Internet Communication Framework.

Step 2: Declare IF_HTTP_EXTENSION~HANDLE_REQUEST in the methods section by keeping it in public visibility.

Step 3: Click on the method and go to source code to deploy the HTML QR scan code in it.

Step 4: To retrieve the HTTP request method of an incoming request in SAP's ICF (Internet Communication Framework) service, attach the below available HTML code for scan QR functionality.

 

METHOD IF_HTTP_EXTENSION~HANDLE_REQUEST.
DATA: lv_request_method TYPE string,
      lv_html           TYPE string,
      lt_html_table     TYPE TABLE OF string,
      gv_json           TYPE char250, "string,
      gs_session        TYPE zpp_tab_sess_id,
      lv_var1           TYPE string,
      lv_var2           TYPE string,
      lv_var3           TYPE string,
      lv_var4           TYPE string,
      lv_var5           TYPE string,
      lv_var6           TYPE string,
      lv_var7           TYPE string,
      lv_var8           TYPE string,
      lv_var9           TYPE string.
      lv_request_method = server->request->get_method( ).
  CASE lv_request_method.
      WHEN 'GET'.
        CLEAR : gv_json.
        lt_html_table = VALUE #(
        ( CONV string( '<!DOCTYPE html>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<html lang="en">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<head>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<meta charset="UTF-8">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<title>QR Code Scanner</title>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<style>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    .message-container {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; margin-top: 20px;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    #statusMessage {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        text-align: center; font-size: 15px; margin-bottom: 5px;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    #rescanButton {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        display: none; margin: 0 auto; padding: 5px 10px; font-size: 10px; cursor: pointer;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</style>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</head>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<body>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<div class="container">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    <h1>Scan QR Codes</h1>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    <div class="section">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        <div style="width: 500px" id="reader" name="QRCodeData"></div>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        <div class="message-container">' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            <p id="statusMessage"></p>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            <button id="rescanButton" onclick="rescan()">Rescan</button>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        </div>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    </div>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</div>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<script src="https://unpkg.com/html5-qrcode"></script>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '<script>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    function onScanSuccess(decodedText, decodedResult) {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const barcodetxt = decodedText;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        console.log(decodedText);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const sessionID = urlParams.get("SESSION_ID") || '''';' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const port = window.location.port;' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const senddata = async (barcodetxt, sessionID, port) => {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            try {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                const res = await fetch(`https://fiori.rmcl.in:${port}/sap/bc/zsrv_evp_qrscan`, {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    method: "POST",' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    headers: {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                        "Content-Type": "application/json"' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    },' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    body: JSON.stringify({ barcodetxt, sessionID })' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                });' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                if (res.ok) {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    document.getElementById("statusMessage").innerText = "Updated Successfully!";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    document.getElementById("statusMessage").style.color = "green";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    setTimeout(() => {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                        window.open("", "_self", "");' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                        window.close();' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    }, 2000);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                } else {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                    showErrorMessage();' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            } catch (error) {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                showErrorMessage();' ) )
        ( CONV string( '                console.error("Error updating data:", error);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '            }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        };' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        senddata(barcodetxt, sessionID, port);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        html5QrcodeScanner.clear();' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    function showErrorMessage() {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const statusMessage = document.getElementById("statusMessage");' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        statusMessage.innerText = "Request failed. Please try again.";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        statusMessage.style.color = "red";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        const rescanButton = document.getElementById("rescanButton");' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        rescanButton.style.display = "inline-block";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    function rescan() {' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        document.getElementById("statusMessage").innerText = "";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        document.getElementById("rescanButton").style.display = "none";' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        html5QrcodeScanner.render(onScanSuccess);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    }' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    var html5QrcodeScanner = new Html5QrcodeScanner(' ) )
        ( CONV string( '        "reader", { fps: 10, qrbox: 250 });' ) )
        ( CONV string( '    html5QrcodeScanner.render(onScanSuccess);' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</script>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</body>' ) )
        ( CONV string( '</html>' ) )
        ).

        CONCATENATE LINES OF lt_html_table INTO lv_html SEPARATED BY cl_abap_char_utilities=>cr_lf.
        server->response->set_cdata( lv_html ).
        server->response->set_status( code = 200 reason = 'OK' ).

      WHEN 'POST'.
        server->request->get_cdata( RECEIVING data = gv_json ).
        lv_html = gv_json.
        server->response->set_content_type( 'text/html' ).
        server->response->set_cdata( lv_html ).
        SPLIT gv_json AT '"' INTO lv_var1  lv_var2 lv_var3 lv_var4 lv_var5 
                                  lv_var6 lv_var7 lv_var8 lv_var9.
        IF lv_var4 IS NOT INITIAL AND lv_var8 IS NOT INITIAL.
          gs_session = VALUE #( session_id = lv_var8
                                qr_value   = lv_var4
                                created_on = sy-datum
                                created_at = sy-uzeit
                                created_by = sy-uname ).
        IF gs_session IS NOT INITIAL.
          MODIFY  zpp_tab_sess_id FROM gs_session.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          COMMIT WORK AND WAIT.
        ELSE.
           ROLLBACK WORK.
        ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
        CLEAR : gv_json,gs_session.
    ENDCASE.
ENDMETHOD.

 

Step 5: After building the logic in se24, bind the class in SICF transaction by giving hierarchy name as SERVICE.

Step 6:  Create a service name in the below navigated hierarchy.

Step 7: Assign the class in which the HTML code has been embedded in the handler list of the created service by double clicking on the service.

Step 8: Create a z-table to store the session id and its data while scanning the QR to avoid the data mismatch.

Step 7: Create a pushbutton in your required screen by using module pool program and name it as Scan QR by keeping its function code as ‘SCAN’

Step 9: In the PAI part write the logic to scan the QR

Code Explanation:

9.1: cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_c32_static( RECEIVING uuid = gv_uuid ) will be used to create GUID’s for the new session while triggering the scan pushbutton.

9.2: gc_srv refers to the service path which is created in SICF t-code along with the session ID .

9.3: gc_host is the HTTPS host link lv_post is the port for the particular server and gv_uuid is the created guid for each session.

 

gc_srv  TYPE string   VALUE '/sap/bc/zsrv_evp_qrscan?SESSION_ID=',
gv_qr         TYPE char50,
gv_uuid       TYPE guid_32.
gv_url        TYPE char200,

 

 

WHEN 'SCAN'.
      CLEAR : gv_qr, gv_uuid.
*****************To get GUID for each new session***********************
      cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_c32_static( RECEIVING uuid = gv_uuid ).
****************Changing the URL according to development and production client*******************
      zcl_ril_constant=>fetch_constant_data(
  EXPORTING
    iv_program_name = 'EVP_PORT'
    iv_field        = 'PORT'
    iv_active_flag  = ''
  IMPORTING
    et_table        = DATA(lt_const)
       ).
      DATA(lv_port) = VALUE #( lt_const[ field_name = 'PORT']-zlow OPTIONAL ).

      CONCATENATE gc_host lv_port gc_srv gv_uuid INTO gv_url.
********************Call Browser triggers the browser for QR scan*********************************
      CALL FUNCTION 'CALL_BROWSER'
        EXPORTING
          url                    = gv_url
          window_name            = 'NEW'
        EXCEPTIONS
          frontend_not_supported = 1
          frontend_error         = 2
          prog_not_found         = 3
          no_batch               = 4
          unspecified_error      = 5
          OTHERS                 = 6.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        CLEAR : gv_url.
        MESSAGE text-069 TYPE 'I'.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE text-072 TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
*******************Mapping the GUID to the Scan QR field*******************************
      SELECT * FROM zpp_tab_sess_id
      INTO TABLE (lt_id)
      WHERE session_id = _uuid.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        DATA(lv_qr_data) = VALUE #( lt_id[ session_id = gv_uuid ]-qr_value OPTIONAL ).
        IF lv_qr_data IS NOT INITIAL .
          gv_qr = lv_qr_data.
        ENDIF.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE text-072 TYPE 'S' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
      ENDIF.

 

Conclusion:

Incorporating QR Code Scanning in a SAP Module Pool with HTML Service offers easy data capture and real-time processing within the SAP GUI. Users can efficiently scan and process QR codes by embedding an HTML page in a Custom Container, using SAP SICF services, and storing scanned data through ABAP methods. This method improves user experience, eliminates human data entry, and enhances system automation.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, public sector Trial System
entry.content:
Curious about SAP's Cloud ERP solution for governmental, non-profit, education and research organizations?  As promised, the new trial environment for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, public sector is now available.  Here's a direct link to the trial system, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, public sector Trial.  You can find the link on the government industries page on SAP.com:

Choose Explore Government Solutions, then select the Trial link under Cloud ERP.

Alternatively, you can also find the link at Try SAP, under Basic Trials, Industries.

The basic trial of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, public sector is a limited-time, 14-day experience that allows you to learn about the solution features and capabilities at no cost and through a shared tenant using sample data. You can explore the system through guided tours or discover functionalities on your own.  

  • Discover the capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for the public sector through guided tours

  • Experience key business processes such as budgeting or grants management and experiment with sample data

  • Start your basic trial without the need for any setup or configuration

You'll find guided tours for Accounts Payable, Grants Management, and Budget Management.

Access the three guided tours from your home page:

  • Grants Management: As a Grants Specialist you would like to monitor grants and review associated details and dimensions. Embedded analytics empowers you to quickly analyze all grants relevant KPIs and alerts. For example, you can see how much budget your grants have and view any open grant relevant commitments.
  • Budget Management: As a Budget Specialist you would like to review details associated with various funds you are managing. You also regularly monitor important information such as budget consumption and open commitments using embedded analytics that empower you to take essential actions and stay on track.
  • Accounts Payable: As an Accountant, you would like to review important KPIs and drill down to the data for analysis and reconciliation. You also regularly identify open invoices to perform further Actions. Embedded analytics help you to easily view details such as overdue payables and cash discount utilization.

Once you've completed the guided tours, click around and check out some of the other available content.   This is just the tip of the iceberg when it comes to the SAP Business Suite functionality available for public sector.  Find links on the Industries or Trial pages to reach out for further information. 

Happy exploring!

entry.title: Key Insights on Flow Builder Plus: Lessons Learned and Future Perspectives
entry.content:
Key Insights on Flow Builder Plus: Lessons Learned and Future Perspectives

Introduction

I would like to share a recent experience with Flow Builder Plus. I participated in Brownfield Migration Projects where companies already had a consolidated cash management process using previous versions of the Liquidity Planner, along with customized reports that were incompatible with S/4HANA 2023.
The project involved detailed mapping of source flows, applying all available SAP Notes until the go-live date, and recovering business-consolidated rules and derivations to ensure the correct classification of cash flows. Additionally, we conducted workshops to define new indicators aligned with business needs.
It was a challenging yet rewarding experience that gave us the chance to explore the full potential of the tool, delivering a fully standard solution that's still evolving to meet the client's needs.

Project Scope

  • Migration to SAP S/4HANA 2023 and scope Advanced Cash Operations (J78_BR)
  • Review of cash flows from the old Liquidity Planner to Flow Builder Plus.
  • Mapping of derivation rules and application of SAP Notes.
  • Ensuring correct classification of cash flows with a 100% standard solution and defining new indicators and reports.

Resources Used

  • SAP Knowledge Base Articles (KBA) as the primary resource for research and troubleshooting.
  • Important Tip: SAP KBA offers solutions to many known Cash Management issues.
  • Highlighted Note: 3454172 - Central KBA for Flow Builder Plus (S/4 HANA Cash Management 2021), which centralizes fixes for various issues.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3454172

Implementation Strategy

  • Activated the Business Function FIN_FSCM_CLM (Full Scope) to integrate liquidity hierarchy with the F2332 (Cash Flow Analyzer) and F0735 (Check Cash Flow Item) apps. (Ensure the client has the compatible license).

  • Applied all available updates to FQM/CLM components, minimizing and restricting future issues.
  • Notable Notes: 3487144, 3359859, 3386510, 3390990, 3394746, 3397150, and many more addressing specific issues.
  • Adjusted configurations to ensure compatibility with Flow Builder Plus.

Reference: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/flow-builder-plus-benefits-basic-details-configurations-etc/ba-p/13572394

  • Reviewed and validated all derivation criteria for liquidity items (FQLINFACC and Queries).

Steps

  1. Bank Account Management
    Some internal management bank accounts were handled via client-customized reports and lacked an Account ID in the system. For these accounts, we configured a Technical Bank using transaction FITE and the app F1366A (Manage Bank Accounts) to ensure the initial balance reflected the defined hierarchies.
  2. Bank Hierarchy Loading
    We loaded the defined bank hierarchy during the project through the Manage Global Hierarchies app - F4973.
  3. Liquidity Hierarchy Loading
    We loaded the defined liquidity hierarchy during the project through the Manage Global Hierarchies app - F4966.
  4. Master Data
    During the project, we identified inconsistencies in data related to the G/L account view (TCODE: FS00 or APP - F0731A).
  5. Rebuild Flow Types in Accounting Documents (BSEG)
    To display and load items in the FQM* tables, the flow type needed to be correctly established for the document items. We executed a full run of transaction TCODE: FQM_UPD_FLOW_TYPE.
  6. Insert Company Bank (BSEG)
    We performed a full load via transaction: FQM_UPD_HBK_HKT to ensure the correct Account ID was populated for document items.
  7. Treasury Level Update
    A treasury level update was performed. The transaction used was: FQM_FD_DPU.
  8. Initial Balance
    Defined the start date for "One Exposure" operations using the VFQM_ACT_STARTDT view, ensuring no "ACTUAL" records before 31/08/2024 were loaded into table FQM_FLOW. From this date onwards, only items created through transaction FQM_INTBAL_AUTOCRT were considered "ACTUAL" during the data process in Flow Builder 2.0.

  9. Rebuild Liquidity Items in Accounting Documents
    We updated the liquidity item with transaction: FQM_UPD_LITEM to ensure proper derivation of liquidity items along with relevant G/L lines.

  10. Flow Builder 2.0
    Finally, we ran the report: FCLM_FLOW_BUILDER_2 to load the records created post-go live and completed the process by activating the job delta triggered by the necessary events.


Conclusion
The goal of this blog was not to detail the step-by-step implementation but to highlight the key points for effective planning and avoiding data issues during the early stages of using the tool.

In future posts, I will detail how we worked on the composition of initial bank balances in conjunction with the liquidity hierarchy to present the medium-term cash forecast.

References

  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Knowledge Base Articles (KBAs)
  • SAP Community
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/flow-builder-plus-benefits-basic-details-configurations-etc/ba-p/13572394
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/flow-builder-plus-the-technical-highlights/ba-p/13523401

entry.title: SAP Cash Application for Contract Accounting: Enhancing FI-CA with AI Capabilities
entry.content:
Challenges with the Clearing of Incoming Payments

SAP Billing and Revenue Management (BRIM) is designed to efficiently handle high transactional volumes through process automation. While configuring business rules enables full automation in most areas, such as billing, invoicing, and collections, many companies still struggle with effective payment allocation. This challenge often arises when relying on arbitrary free text remittance information commonly used in bank transfer payments.

FI-CA Clearing Control is effective only to a certain extent and is highly depended on the discipline of customers in providing accurate remittance information. Although system allows for the configuration of complex and flexible allocation rules through standard configuration and custom code enhancements, predefined business rules cannot address scenarios where a  customer does not follow the prescribed remittance format (e.g. omitting an account number or invoice number) or provides incomplete payment details. As a result, such incoming payments end up in the clarification worklist.

Manual clarification is often time consuming , requiring significant effort, as unresolved clarification cases continue to accumulate. This leads to large amounts of cash being booked in suspense accounts for extended periods, negatively impacting key financial metrics such as available cash flow.

 SAP Cash Application

The AI capabilities embedded in the SAP Cash Application Add-on for Contract Accounting reduce the need for manual intervention and enable the assignment of incoming payments as quickly as possible. Running on the SAP Business Technology Platform, the SAP Cash Application leverages Machine Learning (ML) model to analyse data transferred from the S/4HANA system, identifying selection categories and selection values for FI-CA Clearing Control.

The ML model is trained before productive use, learning from historical clearing information. This learning process continues permanently in the production environment, automatically adapting to changing conditions to keep the model up to date. The key advantage of the ML model is its ability to automatically detect data patterns, while its dynamic learning process ensures long-term effectiveness without requiring additional maintenance effort.

 Key Configuration in Contract Accounting (FI-CA)

  1. Activate Cash Application for the Company Code   

SPRO -> Organizational Units → Set Up Company Codes for Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable

  1. Define Conditions for Using the ML Proposal Based on the Confidence Level Rating

SPRO -> Business Transactions → Payments → Processing Incoming and Outgoing Payments → Incoming Payment Clarification with Machine Learning → Make Settings for SAP Cash Application for Contract Accounting

The configuration determines how the clarification case will be processed in FI-CA based on the confidence rating returned to S/4HANA from the Cash Application. The configuration allows different follow-up processes to be set up:

  • A clearing document will be generated for a matching proposal above the auto-clearing accuracy threshold.
  • Matching proposals under the auto-clearing threshold are shown and displayed for validation.
  • Proposals below the minimum threshold are skipped and not displayed at all.

In this instance (as per the configuration), the payment will be automatically cleared only if the confidence level is very high (greater than 95%). If the confidence level is between 80% and 95%, the system displays the proposal and requires manual post-processing. Below 80%, the system ignores the proposal completely.

Use Case – Payment Clarification with Cash Application

Let’s demonstrate the capabilities of Cash Application for Contract Accouting with a simple use case (note: the screenshots are taken from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition).

In this scenario, a payer has two ongoing service subscription contracts and is paying both invoices at once. The provided note to payee information is: “Payment for Feb 25 subscriptions West Corp” This presents the following challenges for payment allocation:

  • No invoice reference number or account number is provided.
  • The payer name (“West Corp”) may or may not match a Business Partner (BP) ID in the system.

Payment lots are created from the electronic bank statements, and the remittance information provided with the payment will be captured in the note to payee segment of the lot. However, the predefined interpretation rules and configured clearing rules will fail to translate the remittance information into selection categories and values, resulting in the creation of a clarification case.

In a system with the Cash Application Add-on activated, manual processing of clarification cases is blocked until the machine learning (ML) analysis is complete. The system will raise an error message if the user attempts to process the clarification case before the data are sent for analysis.

Next, the clarification case is sent to Cash Application for analysis. This is done through job scheduling using the job template “FI-CA: Transfer Clarification Cases”. In a productive environment, this job should be scheduled immediately after the creation and posting of payment lots to FI-CA.

The ML model applies the logic to find a suitable matching proposal, which is then returned to the S/4HANA system. This process is also done through job scheduling, using the job template “FI-CA: Process Returned and Reversed Clarification Cases”.

The ML model provides matching criteria with a confidence level for each clarification case. Proposals with a confidence level meeting the minimum auto-clearing threshold will trigger the posting of FI-CA document.

Availability

As of now, the Add-On is available for SAP S/4HANA BRIM, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Contract Accounting, and other industry-specific FI-CA variants, including PSCD for Public Services, FS-CD for Insurance Companies, and IS-U for the Utilities sector.

 Further SAP Help references

Cash Application integration for Contract Accounting for Cloud

SAP Cash Application for Contract Accounting: Product information

entry.title: Our Onboarding Solution
entry.content:
Onboarding with the SAP SuccessFactors Solution

 I recently designed and implemented a seamless onboarding journey using SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding in the BizX platform. Here are the key insights:

A well-curated onboarding program is a strategic initiative that enhances new hire productivity, cultural integration and employee retention. Effectiveness of onboarding is a key metric for productivity. Research confirms  that effective onboarding can improve employee performance by up to 15% and increase the likelihood of discretionary effort by 20%, linking onboarding effectiveness to productivity turnaround time. Studies show that incremental improvements in onboarding can lead to a 3x-6x difference in ELTV a topic of great interest to me having participated in the “Stanford continuing studies People Analytics program” and discussed a few related topics.

My  design principles focused on :

My  solution was underpinned by the Cornell design thinking methodology, emphasizing user-centric solutions and iterative development:

A few of the teams involved included :

  • HR: Recruiting, Onboarding support, Payroll
  • IT: Integration, Architecture, Cyber security, BASIS
  • Management: Change management, Project delivery
  • Business: User representatives, Hiring managers
  • External: SAP SuccessFactors consultants

A cross-functional approach ensured comprehensive stakeholder involvement, integrating HR and IT functions to involve all necessary parties.

The solution has several components some of them including:

  • External hire
  • Rehire
  • Cross boarding
  • Offboarding

External hire onboarding solution: Included custom hiring manager tasks using Metadata Framework, complementing standard features.

Rehire onboarding solution:

  • Strategic validation before stakeholder involvement
  • Robust Employee Central (EC) remediation
  • Seamless process implementation
  • Key focus: Termination before rehire for contractor-to-employee conversions

 

Crossboarding solution:

  • Strategic validation before Employee Central record update
  • Seamless process due to cross-module integration
  • Robust EC remediation ensures smooth transitions

Offboarding solution :

  • Designed for simplicity and efficiency
  • Easy for both exiting employees and managers
  • Streamlined to ensure a quick and seamless exit experience

In summary our solution maximizes the platform's potential while addressing unique organizational needs.

For existing Onboarding 1.0 customers:

IBM’s collab with UiPath facilitates a streamlined migration to Onboarding 2.0. This collaboration:

  • Accelerates the transition process
  • Maintains solution integrity
  • Focuses on optimizing key areas
  • Minimizes disruption to existing workflows

The migration will require some reimplementation due to fundamental technical differences between versions. Customers should plan to transition before the 2H 2025 release, as Onboarding 1.0 will enter End of Maintenance phase then, with full deprecation planned for 1H 2026.   

For those interested in a deep dive of the solution details, please feel free to reach out. I'll work on creating a comprehensive blog post to provide more insights and specifics for your benefit.

entry.title: Re: How to set up multi-currency bank?

Hello,

Here's the reply I got from the experts:

  1. What is a “multi-currency bank”? At least when creating new banks via app “Manage Banks – Master Data” there is no currency dependency
  2. “Manage Banks – Cash Management” does not seem to have a currency dependency either
  3. Also creating multiple accounts with different currencies for one bank is also possible via “Manage Bank Accounts”

Kind regards,
Henrike

entry.title: Complex Inbound Process with POSC , LOSC , Quality Inspection in S4 , VAS , Pack Spec and Batch
entry.content:
Curious about how Process-Oriented Storage Control (POSC), Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC), Value-Added Services (VAS), and Quality Inspection (QI) work in SAP S/4HANA EWM?

This blog breaks down the end-to-end inbound delivery process with  scenarios, step-by-step configurations, and testing procedures.

𝗦𝗰𝗲𝗻𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗼 𝗔𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗺𝗽𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻:

An Electronics Manufacturing Vendor is sending 2 Products: Tablets and Smartphones in a single shipment.

𝗧𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲𝘁𝘀: 60 units, packed as 20 per carton, total 3 Carton Boxes.
𝗦𝗺𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗽𝗵𝗼𝗻𝗲𝘀: 150 units.
𝗧𝗼𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗽𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁: 210 units (3 Carton Boxes and 150 Units of Smart Phones) arriving at the Warehouse.

𝗪𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗵𝗼𝘂𝘀𝗲 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝗙𝗹𝗼𝘄:
• 𝗣𝗢𝗦𝗖 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀 (𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀-𝗢𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗦𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗼𝗹)
Steps: Unloading → Deconsolidation → VAS → Final Putaway
• 𝗤𝘂𝗮𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗜𝗻𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 Happens in S/4HANA after Unloading & Goods Receipt (GR) in EWM.
• 𝗣𝗮𝗰𝗸𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗲𝘀𝘀:
Tablets:
• Packed in S/4HANA system .
• Packed in 3 Carton Boxes (ensure consistency).
Smartphones:
Automatically packed in EWM based on Packaging Specification.
• 𝗟𝗢𝗦𝗖 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗧𝗮𝗯𝗹𝗲𝘁𝘀
Tablets follow LOSC after VAS before final Putaway.
• 𝗕𝗮𝘁𝗰𝗵 𝗠𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁
Both Tablets and Smartphones follow Batch Management for traceability.

Flow Chart of the Process:

                                              𝗖𝗼𝗻𝗳𝗶𝗴𝘂𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻:
𝗔𝘀 𝗽𝗲𝗿 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝗰𝗲𝗻𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗼 , 𝘄𝗲 𝗻𝗲𝗲𝗱 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝗳𝗶𝗴𝘂𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗶𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗯𝗲𝗹𝗼𝘄 𝗔𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘀:
• POSC .
• LOSC.
• Putaway Strategies for POSC and LOSC.
• Deconsolidation.
• VAS.
• Quality Management in S4 Side
• Packaging Specification
• Batch Management
• Create two Materials

                                               POSC Configuration:

Spro --> Ref. IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define Process-Oriented Storage Control.

External Storage Process Step:

Storage Process – Definition:

Assign Storage Process Step:

Process-Oriented Storage Control:

Define WPT:

Spro --> Ref. IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define WPT.

 

Determine PTDI:

Spro --> Ref. IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define Control Indicator for Determining WPT.

Determine WPT:

Spro --> Ref. IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Determine WPT.

Work Centre Layout:

SPRO --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Work Centre --> Specify Work Centre Layout.

 

Work Centre:

SPRO --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Work Centre --> Define Work Centre

  • Assign Storage bin in to Decon Workcentre in /n/scwm/tworkst

Deconsolidation Attributes: (Simg-->SCM EWM-->EWM--> GR Process-->Deconsolidation)

  • Define Attributes for Deconsolidation
  • Specify Deconsolidation Station
  • Assign Number Range Intervals to Consolidation Groups

                                                           LOSC Configuration:

Define Storage Type:

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Define Storage type

  • Bin Creation can be using /n/scwm/LS01 or through Bin Structure or Storage bin Upload.
  • Generate Activity Area in the T-code /n/scwm/Gena.
  • Do the Bin Sorting in the T-code /n/scwm/sbst.

LOSC :

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define Layout Oriented Storage Control

Define WPT:

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define WPT

Determine PTDI:

Spro --> Ref. IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define Control Indicator for Determining WPT.

Determine WPT:

Spro --> Ref. IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Determine WPT.

                                       Putaway Strategies for POSC and LOSC:

POSC and LOSC:

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Goods Receipt Process --> Strategies --> Storage Type Search Sequence

Define Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway

           

Assign Storage type to Storage Search Sequence:

      

Define Putaway Control Indicator:

   

Specify Storage Type Search Sequence for Putaway:

POSC:

LOSC:

                                                VAS Configuration:

Work Centre Layout:

SPRO --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Work Centre --> Specify Work Centre Layout.

Work Centre:

SPRO --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Work Centre --> Define Work Centre 

  • Assign Storage bin into VAS Workcentre in /n/scwm/tworkst

Spro --> Ref. IMG --> SCM EWM --> SCM Basis --> Master Data --> Product --> Product Groups:

Define product Group types:

Define Product Group:

SPRO --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Value-Added Services:

Define number Ranges for VAS Order:

Activate Order Document Management for VAS:

Define Relevance for VAS:

Warehouse Number-Dependent VAS Settings:

Packaging Specification for product which undergoes VAS:

Simg --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Packaging Specification --> Define Packaging Specification Group.

Simg --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Packaging Specification --> Maintain Structure for Packaging Specification

Level Type:                                                                                  

    

Element Type:

Level Set:

Work Step:

Define Packaging Material Type:

Assign Packaging Material Type to Element Type:

Assign Work step to Packaging Material type:

Now Create WorkStep and Assign Workstep type in /n/scwm/psworkstep:

Now go to T-code :/n/scwm/packspec:

                                  Quality Management in S4 Side Configuration:

Generate Inspection Object Types Version IOT-04. (We can Use Existing Version Also)

Spro --> IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Basics and Integration -->  Generate Inspection Object Types Version.

Maintain Inspection Object Types Version

Spro --> IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Basics and Integration -->  Maintain Inspection Object Types Version

Define and Activate Warehouse dependent IOT’s

Spro --> IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Basics and Integration -->  Define and Activate Warehouse dependent IOT’s

Maintain Follow-up Actions:

Spro --> Ref IMG -- > SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Inspection Results --> Maintain Follow-up Actions.

Define Follow-up Actions:

Edit Code Groups and Codes for Usage Decisions.

Spro --> Ref IMG -- > SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Quality Management --> Inspection Results --> Edit Code Groups and Codes for Usage Decisions.

ETEWUD01 , ETEWUD03 , ETEWUD04 are copied from Standard Usage Codes 01 , 03 and 04.

Edit Selected Sets for Usage Decisions.

Spro --> Ref IMG --> Quality Management --> Quality Inspection --> Inspection LOT Completion --> Edit Selected Sets for Usage Decisions.

Maintain Settings at Plant Level.

Spro --> Ref IMG --> Quality Management --> Basic Settings --> Maintain Settings at Plant Level.

                                        Packaging Specification Configuration:

Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Packaging Specification.

Number Ranges:

Packaging Specification Group:

Simg --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Packaging Specification --> Maintain Structure for Packaging Specification

Level Type:

Element Type:

Level Set ( Level Type + Element Type):

Assign Level Set to Packaging Specification Group.

Work Step:

Packaging Material Types:

Assign Packaging Material type to Element Type:

Assign Work Step type to Packaging Material type:

Maintain Packaging Work Step: (/n/scwm/psworkstep)

SPRO --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Goods Receipt Process --> Inbound Delivery à Define Document Types for Inbound Delivery Process

Doc Type : INB , Doc Category : PDI

Now create Packaging Specification for the Product /n/scwm/packspec:

                                           Batch Management Configuration:

Spro --> img --> Logistics-General --> Batch-Management --> Specify Batch level and Activate Status Management

Batch Level:

Batch Status Management:

Plant with Batch Status Management:

Spro --> img --> Logistics-General --> Batch-Management --> Batch Number Assignment --> Maintain Internal Batch Number Assignment Range.

Now go to T-code CT04: (Maintain Characteristics):

Now go to T-code CL02 : (Class):

Create 2 Materials :

   

  • Both Material Should be Batch Managed and Quality Inspection Should also be Set-up with Inspection Type 01 , because here Quality Inspection is Done S4 Side.
  • Also , Product Group and Product Group type should also be assigned for VAS Order Creation.
  • Extend Material Location into AFS in MMSC
  • Extend Material Master as Product Master in /n/scwm/Mat1
  • Assign PTDI and PACI.

Now Create Batch Number for the 2 Material in T-code MSC1N.


                                                            Testing:

  • Create a PO in Me21n with Products 6457 and 6458
  • Create Inbound Delivery in Me21n basing on PO:

Now Pack the Material 6457 into 3 Boxes and each box should will have 20 Quantity

So Here Material 6457 is packed and Material 6458 is unpacked.

Save the Delivery and it will be Distributed into EWM Side.

  • Now go to T-code :/n/scwm/PRDI and check the Inbound Delivery

These are the VAS Details Created for VAS Order 5006.

Now Check the Other Product Details and you can See VAS Order is Created for the Other Product also basing on VAS Configuration.

You can See the Below Details for VAS Order 5007.

Now Goto HU Tab and see the HU Details Created.

Now Create the Warehouse Task:

Go to Handling Units Tab :

STPR is the Common Storage Process Followed by POSC and INTR Warehouse Process Type

Now Click on Create+Save.

  • Now Take the Delivery Number and go to Monitor to Process the Remaining Steps:

Now Confirm the Open Warehouse Task’s:

Now in the Below Screenshot Unloading and GR have been completed and Status have changed to C.

  • Now Before confirming the Decon Task’s (Although these are Open Task’s) , the 2 Products here are subjected to Quality Inspection in S4 Side , So go to T-code QA32 and Take the Decision over there.

T-code : QA32 – We can Observe Materials 6457 and 6458 are Subjected to Inspection with Inspection Lot

Now Select the Material and Click on Usage Decision. Now , in this Case we accept the Quality inspection of the Product and Save it.

Once we Select Accept “A” , Follow-up action will be Triggered to take the Decision. Now Do the Quality Inspection in the Same way for the Other material 6458 and in this Case Accept the Quality of the Material and Save it.

  • Now Come back to the Monitor and Refresh it:

Now Confirm the Warehouse Task in Background for Deconsolidation:

Now go to Decon Work Centre to Confirm the Task. (T-code : /n/scwm/DCONS)

 Select the HU number and Click on the ICON as shown above (ICON Indicates the HU Process Complete). After Clicking on ICON , Save it and you can see the Task which was created by system in the above image. This is how we confirm the Task in Deconsolidation Work Centre.

Now Go back to Monitor and Refresh the Screen . Once you refresh , System creates another open Warehouse Task which is called VAS

Now Confirm the WT’s in Background:

Now VAS WT’s have been Confirmed as shown in the Below image.

Now got VAS workcentre to confirm the Task ( T-code : /n/scwm/VASEXEC)

Go to VAS Activity Tab and give the VAS Order Number , VAS Status , Activity and Completed Activity and Click on Execute, Now VAS Order will be changed.

After this Click on the ICON complete HU Process and Save it . Now the VAS Activity will be completed.

For Reference , you can check the VAS Services in the Desktop for each Product for which VAS is created . Here in the below Images , for the VAS Order 5006 and 5007, System is Showing VAS Activity has been completed , since it is confirmed in the VAS Work Centre.

    

Now Comeback to Monitor and Refresh it. You can Observe the Product 6458 is in VAS Bin

For the Product 6457 which is in VAS Bin, Once we confirm this task , it will be moved to Intermediate Bin which is LOSC-BIN , since it is subjected to LOSC process , before the final Putaway , since the product has to be kept in high Rack Storage type (Mezzanine Storage type).

Now Confirm the Open WT’s:

You can Observe the Product 6458 has been moved to Final Storage Bin , and the Product 6457 has been moved to Intermediate Storage bin which is LOSC-Bin.

Now Confirm the Open WT’s for the Product 6457 and once it is confirmed , the Products are placed in the final Storage Bin as shown below

 Now Come back and Refresh the Desktop , you can see the Warehouse Activity has been Completed

Conclusion:

With the right configurations and structured execution, SAP EWM Inbound Delivery Processing can be optimized for efficiency and traceability. I hope this guide helps you gain a deeper understanding of POSC, LOSC, VAS, and QI in S/4HANA. Let me know your thoughts and experiences in the comments!

        

 

 

 

 

 

 

:

entry.title: Familiarize with Future of Analytics “SAP Business Data Cloud “
entry.content:
Overview :

SAP's strategic New solution called SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) was created to assist businesses in integrating, managing, and analyzing data from a variety of sources, including SAP and non-SAP systems. It offers a cutting-edge framework for AI, business analytics, and decision-making.

Today's Customer Requirements are :

  • Easier access to SAP Data

Challenge : Customers have to extract data out of SAP Systems and rebuild the semantic and context, to prepare data for analytics and AI.

  • Access All data across line of business

Challenge : Access to sap Line of business data is inconsistent and requires 3rd party connectors and tools.

  • Unified Business View

Challenges : Customers wants a unified view of their business. But each sap applications has its own data model. Data Harmonization is very difficult.

  • Accelerate AI with AI-ready data

Challenges : Customers want to accelerate innovation with AI. But lacks the high-quality data needed for reliable and accurate AI.

  • Faster time to Insights

Challenges : Customers need to invest time and resources to build their own semantic models, analytic and data rich applications.

  • Simplified Landscape:

Challenges : Multiple tools, silos and manual data preparation slows down development of advanced analytics.

SAP's Answer to the above challenges : Business Data Cloud (BDC) :

Key Benefits of SAP Business Data Cloud

- Unified Data Landscape – Seamlessly integrates SAP and non-SAP data without replication.
- Improved Data Governance – Ensures compliance, security, and data consistency.
- Business-Centric Data Models – Translates raw data into meaningful business insights.
- Enhanced Collaboration – Enables secure data sharing across organizations and partners.
- Faster Decision-Making – Leverages real-time analytics and AI-driven insights.
- Scalability & Flexibility – Works with various cloud environments and grows with business needs.

There are many key terminologies we will be learning on this journey , In this section we will take only 2 key concepts in SAP Business data cloud : 

1. Data Product

2. Insight Apps

Data Product :

A data product is a well-organized, reusable, and packed dataset that can be used in a variety of business processes, analytics programs, and applications. Across systems, it guarantees controlled, high-quality, and trusted data.

Key Characteristics:

  • Standardized & reusable across multiple use cases.
  • Includes metadata, lineage, and governance controls.
  • Can be shared internally (within the organization) or externally (partners, vendors).

Insight Apps :

An insight app is an interactive analytical tool that uses data from multiple sources to deliver business-driven insights in real time. It is intended to facilitate operational effectiveness and decision-making.

Key Characteristics:

  • Built with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).
  • Uses interactive dashboards, KPIs, and AI-driven insights.
  • Often embedded within operational systems like SAP S/4HANA.
  • Helps users take action based on insights (e.g., triggering a workflow).

Conclusion:

SAP Business Data Cloud offers a complete framework for contemporary businesses to effectively manage and use their data. Businesses may increase transparency, operational effectiveness, and creativity in the rapidly changing digital economy by embracing this platform. we will learn more about this in coming days.

Thank you ! Happy Learning !

entry.title: Re: Best DMS on-premise backend for SAP HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I think the idea of using SharePoint on premise is a very good idea.

We have solution that can "bridge the gap" between S4HC and SharePoint, in practice it does not work as CMIS storage because that has many limitations regarding folders generation and attributes in SharePoint.

Our solution can connect to S4HC for getting attachments and data, and is then creating folder structures in SharePoint, storing files and adding attributes. In S4HC it replaces the real attachments with URL to the SharePoint folders.

A "flavour" of it is used today in production by a customer, connecting C4C (the principle is the same as for S4HC, using existing APIs) to SharePoint on-prem.

So we would work in a collaborative way if you want to proceed further with this.  Let me know in private message if you're interested
